question: How many days of paid annual leave are full-time employees entitled to?
ground_truth_answer: Full-time employees are entitled to 20 days of paid annual leave per calendar year.
relevant_chunks: Employees are entitled to 20 days of paid annual leave per calendar year.
Full-time employees: 20 days per year, prorated for part-time staff.

question: What happens to carried-over leave days if they are not used within the first 90 days?
ground_truth_answer: Carried-over leave days must be used within the first 90 days of the new year or they will expire.
relevant_chunks: Any carried-over days must be used within the first 90 days of the new year or they will expire.

question: What is the entitlement for paid sick leave per year?
ground_truth_answer: Employees are entitled to 10 days of paid sick leave per year.
relevant_chunks: Sick Leave: Employees are entitled to 10 days of paid sick leave per year; long-term sickness requires a medical certificate and coordination with HR.

question: Within how many days should expense reports be submitted?
ground_truth_answer: Expense reports should be submitted within 30 days of the expense date.

question: What happens to expense reports submitted after 60 days?
ground_truth_answer: Expenses submitted after 60 days require manager justification and may be declined.

question: What approval is required for expenses above $500?
ground_truth_answer: Expenses above $500 require manager and finance approval before reimbursement.
